definition: A tangible object as the object-side correlate of body contact.
notes: The project prefers tangible because it is concise and works naturally in the standard sense-base formulas. Touchable is acceptable when the English line benefits from something more plainly adjectival or less technical. The OSF glossary confirms tangible as the house default and records touchable as a tolerated alternate.

context_rules.context: In most analytical and source-facing contexts
In explanatory prose where a plainer adjective is helpful

example_phrases.notes: Object-side term in standard sense-base formulas.

authority_basis.scope: Supports keeping tangible as the house default for tactile objects within the sixfold analysis.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use 'tangible' as the default rendering in the entry's governed translation contexts unless a recorded context rule overrides it.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Yield to recorded context_rules, source-facing technical use, or other explicitly documented local reasons rather than rotating among alternates ad hoc.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Prevents drift back toward 'object' or other unrecorded alternates.
